& \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{w}}{150 \times 10^3} \times 10^6=12 \\

\end{aligned}$

& \Rightarrow \mathrm{w}_1=8.935 \mathrm{gm}
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Mass percentage of $\text{Fe} = 69.9\%$

Mass percentage of $\text{O} = 100\% - 69.9\% = 30.1\%$

Number of moles of $\text{Fe} = \dfrac{69.9}{56} = 1.248$

Number of moles of $\text{O} = \dfrac{30.1}{16} = 1.881$

Molar ratio of $\text{Fe} : \text{O} = 1.248 : 1.881$

Dividing by the smallest value, the ratio is $\dfrac{1.248}{1.248} : \dfrac{1.881}{1.248} = 1 : 1.5$

Multiplying by $2$ to get the simplest whole number ratio, we get $2 : 3$.

The empirical formula is $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$.

Answer: $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
All nonzero digits are significant. All zeros that are found between nonzero digits are significant. Leading zeros (to the left of the first nonzero digit) are not significant. Trailing zeros for a whole number that ends with a decimal point are significant.The number of significant figures of the given numbers are as follows:
Numbers                   No. of significant figures
(A) 0.00253                 3  
(B) 1.0003                   5
(C) 15.0                        3
(D) 163                         3
∴ (A), (C) and (D) have the same number of significant figures.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Molar masses: HCl = 36.5, CaCO₃ = 100 g/mol
HCl solution: mass = 300 × 1.13 = 339 g
HCl present: 339 × 0.3855 = 130.74 g = 130.74/36.5 = 3.58 mol
CaCO₃: 90/100 = 0.9 mol
Reaction: $CaCO_3 + 2HCl → CaCl_2 + H_2O + CO_2$
HCl required: 0.9 × 2 = 1.8 mol
HCl available: 3.58 mol > 1.8 mol
CaCO₃ is limiting. HCl remaining: (3.58 - 1.8) × 36.5 = 1.78 × 36.5 = 64.97 g

Correct answer is $12$

solution:
Volume of silver coating =0.05×0.05×10000=25 cm3

Given, Density =7.9 g cm-3

Density = Mass/ Volume 

Mass of silver deposited =25×7.9 g

Moles of silver atoms = mass / molar mass =25×7.9108

Number of silver atoms =25×7.9108×6.023×1023

=11.01×1023

Ans. 11

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Reaction: A + 2B → AB₂.
Moles of A = 36.0/60 = 0.6 mol;
Moles of B = 56.0/80 = 0.7 mol.
A requires 1.2 mol of B but only 0.7 available, so B is limiting reagent.
So, (A) False - B is limiting.
(B) AB₂ formed = 0.7/2 = 0.35 mol;
Molar mass AB₂ = 60 + 2(80) = 220 g/mol;
Mass = 0.35 × 220 = 77 g True.
(C) False - Molar mass is 220 g/mol.
(D) A remaining = 0.6 - 0.35 = 0.25 mol = 15 g True.
Correct statements: B and D.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Organic compound $\xrightarrow{\text { combustion }} \underset{\substack{\mathrm{0.9} \mathrm{gm}}}{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}$
$\therefore$ mole of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}=\frac{0.9}{18}=0.05 \mathrm{~mole}$
$\therefore$ mole of H in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}=0.05 \times 2=0.1$ mole
$=$ mole of H in 0.01 mole
Organic compound
$\therefore$ wt of H atom in 0.01 mole compound $=0.1 \times 1$ $=0.1 \mathrm{gm}$


$\therefore$ wt of H atom in one mole compound
$=\frac{0.1}{0.01}=10 \mathrm{gm}$
$\because \mathrm{wt} . \%$ of $\mathrm{H}=\frac{\mathrm{wt} \text {. of } \mathrm{H} \text { in one mole compound }}{\text { Molar mass of compound }} \times 1$
$\begin{aligned}
& 10=\frac{10}{M} \times 100 \\
& \therefore M=100
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
N2g+3H2g⇌2NH3g20 g       5 g                         Given mass2028         52                          Number of moles

To find the limiting reagent we can divide given number of moles with Stoichiometric coefficients, whichever will be the lower will be limiting reagent.

N2→20281=2028  H2→523=56

∴ N2 is the Limiting Reagent.

According to the reaction one mole of nitrogen gas gives two moles of ammonia.

∴ nNH3=2×nN2=2×2028

Number of moles of ammonia formed will be =1.42

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_{10}+\frac{13}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 4 \mathrm{CO}_2+5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} \\
& 3 \times 10^3 \quad 10 \times 10^3
\end{aligned}$
Moles of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ formed $=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}=5 \times \frac{2}{13} \times 10 \times 10^3$
Then $\mathrm{w}_{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}}=\frac{10^5}{13} \times 18$
$=1.3846 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~g}$
Volume of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ will be $=138.46$ litre.
Ans. 138

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Number of atoms is calculated as the product of the number of moles, atomicity, and Avogadro's number ($N_A$).

For A: Moles of O$_2$ = $\dfrac{2}{32} = \dfrac{1}{16}$ mol. Atomicity of O$_2$ is $2$.
Number of atoms = $\dfrac{1}{16} \times 2 \times N_A = \dfrac{1}{8} N_A$

For B: Moles of SO$_2$ = $\dfrac{4}{64} = \dfrac{1}{16}$ mol. Atomicity of SO$_2$ is $3$.
Number of atoms = $\dfrac{1}{16} \times 3 \times N_A = \dfrac{3}{16} N_A$

For C: Moles of O$_2$ at STP = $\dfrac{1400}{22400} = \dfrac{1}{16}$ mol. Atomicity of O$_2$ is $2$.
Number of atoms = $\dfrac{1}{16} \times 2 \times N_A = \dfrac{1}{8} N_A$

For D: Moles of He at STP = $\dfrac{0.05}{22.4} = \dfrac{1}{448}$ mol. Atomicity of He is $1$.
Number of atoms = $\dfrac{1}{448} \times 1 \times N_A = \dfrac{1}{448} N_A$

For E: Moles of H$_2$ = $0.0625 = \dfrac{1}{16}$ mol. Atomicity of H$_2$ is $2$.
Number of atoms = $\dfrac{1}{16} \times 2 \times N_A = \dfrac{1}{8} N_A$

Comparing the number of atoms, A, C, and E contain the same number of atoms.

Answer: A, C and E only

solution:
Let hydrocarbon is CxHy

10x=40 ; x=4 and 10x+y4=55
x+y4=5510
x+y4=5.5
Put the value of x
4+y4=5.5
y4=5.5-4
y4=1.5 then y=6
Hence hydrocarbon is C4H6

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Let the formula of metal chloride be MClx

Mass of 100 mL of vapours of MClx at STP =0.57 g

The mass of substance which occupies 22400 ml of volume at STP is molar mass(M)

∴ 100×M22400=0.57

∴ Molar mass of MClx=127.68 g mol–1

The mass percentage of chlorine=mass of chlorinemass of the compound×100

% of Cl=35.5x×100127.68=55

⇒ x=2

∴ Formula of metal chloride is MCl2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
CH4+2O2→CO2+2H2O

Gram molecular mass of CO2=12+2(16)=44 g/mol

From the reaction, it is clear that 1 mole of methane on complete combustion produces 44 g (1 mole) of carbon dioxide.

Therefore moles of CH4 required to produce 22 g of CO2 are:

=144×22=0.5 mol =50×10-2 mol

Hence, m=50

After combustion, total volume
345=VN2+VCO2

x=3
Volume of O2 used

y=8
So, hydrocarbon isC3H8.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
No. of moles of oxygen in $0.16 \mathrm{~g}$ of oxygen molecule
$=\frac{0.16 \mathrm{~g}}{32 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}}=0.005 \mathrm{~mole}$
$2 \mathrm{NaClO}_3 \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} 2 \mathrm{NaCl}+3 \mathrm{O}_2$
According to the reaction, 
$3$ moles of $\mathrm{O}_2=2$ moles of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ 
$=2$ moles of $\mathrm{AgCl}$
Molar mass of $\mathrm{AgCl}=143.5 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol}$
$0.005$ moles of $\mathrm{O}_2$ will ppt. $=0.005 \times \frac{2}{3}$ moles $\mathrm{AgCl}$
$=0.0033 \text { moles of } \mathrm{AgCl}$
$\therefore$ Mass of $\mathrm{AgCl}$ (in $\mathrm{g}$) obtained will be 
$=143.5 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{mol} \times 0.0033$ moles $=0.48 \mathrm{~g}$.
